title: Rigveda and Tarikh-e-Khandan-e-Timuriyah
focus: Covers the foundational Vedic text and the 16th-century Mughal illustrated manuscript commissioned by Akbar.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 017]
The Rigveda (Successfully inscribed in 2007)
* One of the oldest literary texts, the Rigveda is preserved on birch bark and paper in New Delhi and Pune.
* It contains hymns, myths, and rituals, forming the foundation of Vedic science, yoga, Ayurveda, and Sanskrit traditions.

[Page 018]
The Tarikh-e-Khandan-e-Timuriyah (Successfully inscribed in 2011)
* A 16th-century illustrated manuscript, commissioned by Emperor Akbar in 1577-78 A.D, documenting the history of Timur and his Mughal descendants.
* It features 133 exquisite Mughal miniature paintings by court artists like Daswant, Basawan, and Manohar.
